data.title: ZHEN DING RESOURCES INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Zhen Ding Resources Inc., a company primarily engaged in the processing of metal ore and selling ore concentrates of gold, silver, lead, zinc, and copper, has released its Form 10-Q report for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in financial performance and outlines key operational developments and future plans.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: The company reported a net income of $429,117 for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to a net loss of $460,935 for the same period in 2024. This improvement is primarily due to a gain on the extinguishment of debt.
  • Net Income (Loss) for Three Months Ended September 30, 2025: The net loss for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was $(162,828), a decrease of 4.8% compared to the same period in 2024. This reduction is attributed to a modest decrease in general and administrative expenses and interest expenses.
  • Net Income Per Share: The basic and diluted net income per common share for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, was $0.00, reflecting the company's improved financial performance.
  • Operating Loss for Three Months Ended September 30, 2025: The operating loss for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was $(41,038), showing a decrease compared to the same period in 2024, indicating effective cost management efforts.
  • Operating Loss for Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025: The operating loss for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, was $(76,499), a decrease compared to the same period in 2024, indicating improved operational efficiency.
  • Interest Expenses for Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025: Interest expenses were $371,241, showing a slight decrease from the previous year, reflecting reduced financial costs.
  • General and Administrative Expenses for Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025: General and administrative expenses were $76,499, a decrease of 11.2% from the previous year, indicating effective cost control measures.

Business Highlights

  • Geographical Performance: The company's operations are primarily based in China, focusing on the processing of metal ore and selling ore concentrates of gold, silver, lead, zinc, and copper. The joint venture, Zhen Ding JV, is equipped to process ore mined by its partner at the Wuxi Gold Mine.
  • Operational Challenges: The company has faced challenges due to the idling of its mineral processing plant, which was initially shut down in 2017 due to insufficient working capital. This has resulted in no revenue generation during the reporting period.
  • Recent Developments: On December 18, 2024, the Anhui Province Jing County People's Court approved the bankruptcy of the joint venture partner, Xinzhou Gold. Subsequently, the 30% share of the joint venture was transferred to Mr. Wei De Gang, ensuring the continuation of operations under his ownership.
  • Future Outlook: The company is in preliminary discussions with Xinan Environmental Protection (XEP) to develop a waste-to-energy power generation operation using the Wuxi Gold Mine Lands and infrastructure. This collaboration is subject to feasibility study results, negotiation of terms, and government approval.
  • Plan of Operation: The company plans to seek an investment of approximately US$3,350,000 to resume mineral extraction and refinery activities. It is also actively seeking partnerships with mining enterprises to increase its supply of raw material and enhance its capability to access necessary funding.
  • Operational Strategy: The company intends to focus on partnerships with companies in the exploration and extraction of precious and base metals, primarily in China, to provide management expertise and assist in financing efforts. This strategy aims to acquire metal ores for processing and selling ore concentrates to metal refineries.

SEC Filing: ZHEN DING RESOURCES INC. [ RBTK ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: READING INTERNATIONAL INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Reading International Inc., a diversified company primarily engaged in the cinema and real estate sectors, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities during the period, providing insights into its revenue streams, geographical performance, and future outlook.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $52.17 million for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, reflecting a decrease from $60.09 million in the same period in 2024, primarily due to lower cinema revenue.

Operating Income (Loss): $(329) thousand for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(343) thousand in the same period in 2024, indicating a slight improvement despite lower revenues.

Net Income (Loss): $(4.31) million for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(7.14) million in the same period in 2024, showing a reduction in net loss.

Basic EPS: $(0.18) for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.31) in the same period in 2024, reflecting an improvement in earnings per share.

Diluted EPS: $(0.18) for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.31) in the same period in 2024, consistent with basic EPS.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The cinema segment generated revenue from admissions, concessions, and advertising across the United States, Australia, and New Zealand. The real estate segment earned revenue through property rentals and live theatre operations in the United States, Australia, and New Zealand.

Geographical Performance: In the United States, cinema admissions revenue was $13.8 million for the quarter, with concessions revenue at $9.3 million. In Australia, admissions revenue was $12.4 million, and in New Zealand, it was $1.8 million. The real estate segment in the United States generated $1.95 million in revenue, while Australia contributed $2.39 million.

Sales Units: The cinema segment reported admissions revenue of $40.1 million in the United States, $36.3 million in Australia, and $5.7 million in New Zealand for the nine months ended September 30, 2025.

Future Outlook: Management anticipates improvements in cinema patronage and operating revenue levels with an increase in the quantity and quality of films being released. The company plans to raise liquidity through real estate asset monetizations and refinancing efforts to address going concern uncertainties.

SEC Filing: READING INTERNATIONAL INC [ RDI ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: MacKenzie Realty Capital, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
MacKenzie Realty Capital, Inc., a company specializing in leasing office space and apartment units, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, highlighting significant improvements in financial performance and operational developments. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial health and business activities for the recent quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $4.54 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous year's $4.95 million, primarily due to lower rental income.
  • Operating Loss: $(3.49) million, improved from the previous year's $(7.44) million, indicating better cost management and reduced impairment losses.
  • Net Income: $(3.05) million, a significant improvement from the previous year's $(7.41) million, driven by reduced operating expenses and unrealized gains on equity securities.
  • Net Loss Attributable to Common Stockholders: $(4.09) million, compared to $(8.14) million in the prior year, showing a narrowing of losses.
  • Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Share: $(2.20), improved from $(6.10) in the previous year, reflecting the impact of the reverse stock split and reduced net loss.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company generates rental revenue by leasing office space and apartment units to tenants. These leases are classified as operating leases, and revenues are recognized on a straight-line basis over the lease terms.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates several properties across different locations, including Oakland, CA; Hollywood, CA; Concord, CA; Duluth, GA; Napa, CA; Woodland, CA; Fairfield, CA; and Suisun, CA. Each location has varying numbers of tenants and property types, contributing to the overall operational performance.
  • Sales Units: The company manages multiple properties with a total of 14 tenants at the Green Valley Medical Center and 14 tenants at Aurora at Green Valley, indicating a stable occupancy rate across its portfolio.
  • New Production Launches: The company completed the construction of Aurora at Green Valley, which consists of three residential buildings and a clubhouse. The clubhouse opened in June 2025, and the first residential building was completed in July 2025, with leasing commencing in August 2025. The remaining two buildings were completed in August and September 2025.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to commence construction of the Blue Ridge at Suisun Valley in spring 2026, subject to city approval and securing necessary financial resources. This development is expected to enhance the company's portfolio and operational capacity.

SEC Filing: MacKenzie Realty Capital, Inc. [ MKZR ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Lightstone Value Plus REIT IV, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Lightstone Value Plus REIT IV, Inc., a real estate investment trust focused on real estate and real estate-related investments, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities for the period ended September 30, 2025.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $8.2 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $8.5 million for the same period in 2024. The decrease was primarily due to a reduction in food, beverage, and other revenues.

Net Loss: $(374) thousand for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(1,369) thousand for the same period in 2024. The improvement was driven by a decrease in interest expense and a net casualty gain.

Net Loss Per Company’s Common Shares, Basic and Diluted: $(0.05) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.14) for the same period in 2024.

Total Revenue: $21.9 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $21.3 million for the same period in 2024. The increase was due to higher room revenues reflecting improved occupancy and ADR.

Net Loss: $(3,979) thousand for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(8,165) thousand for the same period in 2024. The reduction in net loss was attributed to lower interest expenses and a net casualty gain.

Net Loss Per Company’s Common Shares, Basic and Diluted: $(0.39) for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.79) for the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

[Hotel Operations]: The Williamsburg Moxy Hotel, a 216-room Marriott branded hotel located in Brooklyn, New York, experienced a high occupancy rate of 91% for the nine months ended September 30, 2025. The hotel reported a RevPAR of $244.77 and an ADR of $270.11, indicating strong performance in room revenue.

[Casualty Event and Recovery]: The Williamsburg Moxy Hotel suffered damage from a fire in December 2024, affecting one of its food and beverage venues. The hotel has filed an insurance claim and received advances totaling $1.5 million, with expectations of further recoveries related to business interruption.

[Joint Venture Developments]: The Williamsburg Moxy Hotel Joint Venture, in which the company holds a 75% interest, continues to be a significant part of operations. Lightstone REIT III holds the remaining 25% interest, and the joint venture is managed by the company.

[Real Estate Development]: The 40 East End Ave. Joint Venture, in which the company holds a 33.3% interest, has sold 27 out of 29 units in its luxury residential condominium project in Manhattan, New York. The remaining two units are expected to contribute to future cash flows.

[Future Outlook]: The company anticipates that cash flows from the Williamsburg Moxy Hotel and potential sales from the 40 East End Ave. Joint Venture will be sufficient to meet operational and financial obligations over the next 12 months. The company is also considering additional capital contributions from Lightstone REIT III if necessary.

[Operational Strategy]: The company continues to focus on real estate and real estate-related investments, including operating properties and development projects. The strategy includes potential investments in mezzanine loans, mortgage loans, and preferred equity interests, with a focus on development-related opportunities.

SEC Filing: Lightstone Value Plus REIT IV, Inc. [ LTSV ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: GOOD GAMING, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Good Gaming, Inc., a company previously known for its involvement in Minecraft server management and esports tournaments, has released its Q3 2023 10-Q report. The report highlights significant changes in the company's business strategy and provides a detailed overview of its financial performance during the reporting period. The company is now focusing on the mobile gaming market through a new partnership with ViaOne Services, marking a strategic pivot from its previous operations.

Financial Highlights

Revenues: $0, reflecting a decrease of $174 or 100% compared to the same period in 2024 due to the decision to halt Minecraft development.

Gross Loss: $(18,689), indicating a reduction in revenue-generating activities.

Operating Loss: $(49,669), a decrease of $140,853 or 73.91% from the previous year, attributed to reduced operating expenses.

Net Loss: $(50,675), a decrease of $127,594 or 71.57% from the previous year, primarily due to a decrease in operating expenses.

Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Common Share: $(0.00), consistent with the previous year, reflecting the company's ongoing financial challenges.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company has halted its Minecraft development, which contributed to a decrease in revenue for the reporting period.

New Product Launches: The company is focusing on pre-installing games on mobile devices through a partnership with ViaOne Services. This represents a shift in business strategy towards mobile gaming distribution.

Operational Strategy: The company has exited its previous business operations, including managing Minecraft servers and hosting esports tournaments, to focus on distributing mobile games by pre-installing them on devices sold by mobile phone service providers.

Future Outlook: The company plans to create partnerships with game developers to pre-install games on thousands of devices, measure the results through controlled tests, and seek additional game developers and publishers for player acquisition.

Operational Changes: The company has ceased operations on certain projects that were not profitable, leading to a reduction in operating expenses and a focus on new strategic initiatives.

Business Focus: The company is at an early stage of its new business operations, emphasizing the need for additional capital to fund its activities and achieve its operational milestones.

SEC Filing: GOOD GAMING, INC. [ GMER ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: BrooQLy Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
BrooQLy Inc., now operating as Dynamic Aerospace Systems Corporation, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025. The report highlights the company's strategic shift towards UAV manufacturing and autonomous logistics, following significant acquisitions and rebranding efforts. This transition marks a pivotal moment for the company as it aims to establish itself in the aerospace and logistics sectors.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $0 - The company generated no revenue for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025, reflecting its transition to a new business model focused on UAV manufacturing and autonomous logistics.
  • Loss from operations: $(1.1) million - The loss from operations for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was primarily due to increased professional fees and general and administrative costs associated with the acquisition and integration of new business assets.
  • Net loss: $(1.3) million - The net loss for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was driven by operating expenses and interest and other expenses, reflecting the company's investment in scaling operations and preparing for future growth.
  • Net Loss Per Common Stock - basic and fully diluted: $(0.04) - The net loss per share increased due to higher operating expenses and interest costs associated with the company's strategic acquisitions and business expansion.

Business Highlights

  • Segment Operations: BrooQLy Inc., now operating as Dynamic Aerospace Systems Corporation, has transitioned its focus to two main segments: Dynamic Aerospace Systems (DAS) and Dynamic Deliveries. DAS is involved in the manufacturing of UAVs, while Dynamic Deliveries focuses on autonomous mesh logistics networks.
  • Business Transition: The company rebranded to Dynamic Aerospace Systems to align with its strategic focus on UAV innovation and logistics, following the acquisition of assets from Vayu (US) Inc., Impossible Aerospace Corporation, and Global Autonomous Corporation.
  • Operational Focus: The company is concentrating on developing and commercializing advanced VTOL drones and UAV systems, such as the G1 and US-1, for applications in autonomous logistics, surveillance, and reconnaissance.
  • Geographical Performance: The company serves government, defense, and commercial sectors across the United States, Gulf Coast nations, and NATO countries, focusing on optimizing efficiency and reducing risk through autonomous aerial solutions.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates that commercial activity from acquired assets will begin offsetting operating costs over the coming quarters. The company is also planning to expand its manufacturing capabilities and pursue additional contracts with government and commercial clients.
  • Strategic Investments: Significant investments have been made in R&D, manufacturing, and operational costs, particularly in enhancing proprietary UAV designs with autonomous flight controls and advanced sensor integration.
  • Board Expertise: The appointment of Jorge L. Torres, a FedEx logistics expert, to the Board of Directors is expected to enhance the company's strategic expertise in logistics and supply chain optimization.
  • Future Developments: The company plans to conduct flight testing at Strother Field, Kansas, expected to be operational in 2026, and is exploring further equity and debt financing to support ongoing R&D and expansion efforts.

SEC Filing: BrooQLy Inc. [ BRQL ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: BANK OF THE JAMES FINANCIAL GROUP INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Bank of the James Financial Group Inc., a prominent financial institution based in Central Virginia, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements, reflecting its resilience and strategic growth in a challenging economic environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $34.643 million, reflecting an increase from $33.007 million in the same period last year, driven by higher loan and securities income.
  • Net Interest Income: $24.269 million, up from $21.550 million, indicating improved interest margins.
  • Net Income: $6.299 million, slightly down from $6.326 million, with stable performance despite economic challenges.
  • Net Income Per Common Share - Basic and Diluted: $1.39, consistent with the previous year, showing steady earnings per share.
  • Total Noninterest Income: $11.527 million, a slight increase from $11.321 million, supported by gains on loan sales and wealth management fees.
  • Total Noninterest Expenses: $28.441 million, up from $25.602 million, primarily due to increased salaries, professional expenses, and FDIC insurance costs.
  • Provision for (Recovery of) Credit Losses: $(301) thousand, reflecting a recovery compared to $(584) thousand last year, indicating improved credit conditions.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reports three business segments: Community Banking, Mortgage Banking, and Investment Advisory. Community Banking provides loans, deposits, and related services, generating revenue primarily from net interest income. Mortgage Banking focuses on originating residential mortgage loans for sale, with revenue mainly from gains on loan sales. Investment Advisory offers financial planning services, with revenue primarily fee-based, tied to assets under management.
  • Geographical Performance: The primary market area for the company is Central Virginia, including the Town of Altavista, Amherst County, Appomattox County, the Town of Bedford, Bedford County, Campbell County, and the City of Lynchburg. The company has expanded into other areas in Virginia, such as Roanoke, Charlottesville, Harrisonburg, Blacksburg, Lexington, Rustburg, Buchanan, and Nellysford.
  • Sales Units: The Community Banking segment reported total loans held for investment, net of allowance, at $653.3 million as of September 30, 2025, compared to $636.6 million at December 31, 2024. Deposits totaled $922.1 million at September 30, 2025, up from $882.4 million at December 31, 2024.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates that the ongoing application of updated credit loss models, along with normal portfolio dynamics, updated forecasts, and loan growth trends, will continue to influence the provision for credit losses. The company remains focused on maintaining adequate liquidity and managing credit quality amid economic uncertainties.
  • Investment Advisory Performance: Assets under management for the Investment Advisory segment were $984.7 million at September 30, 2025, showing growth from $854.0 million at December 31, 2024, and $842.8 million at September 30, 2024.

SEC Filing: BANK OF THE JAMES FINANCIAL GROUP INC [ BOTJ ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Fitch Affirms A.I. Candelaria's Ratings at 'BB'; Outlook Negative
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Chicago-14 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has affirmed A.I. Candelaria (Spain), S.A.'s (A.I. Candelaria) Long-Term Foreign and Local Currency Issuer Default Ratings and USD950 million of notes outstanding due between 2028 and 2033 at 'BB'. The Rating Outlook is Negative.

A.I. Candelaria's ratings remain linked to the credit profile of its sole cash flow source Oleoducto Central S.A. (OCENSA; BB+/Negative) and, indirectly, to Ecopetrol S.A. (BB+/Negative) which owns 72.65% of OCENSA. Dependence on dividends from a minority stake in OCENSA continues to be the key driver of A.I. Candelaria's credit profile.

The ratings also incorporate the significant influence of A.I. Candelaria on OCENSA's dividend policy, which mitigates the dependence on a single source of cash flow from its minority interest in OCENSA. The ratings are constrained by A.I. Candelaria's moderately high leverage and structural subordination to OCENSA's creditors.

Key Rating Drivers

Adequate Dividend Stream: The ratings are supported by the quality and stability of dividends received from A.I. Candelaria's 27.35% interest in OCENSA. OCENSA operates a regulated business with strong cash flow generation and a good track record of distributions. Fitch's base case assumes dividend receipts of USD180 million-USD193 million over the rating horizon. OCENSA operates Colombia's largest crude oil pipeline, connecting key basins to the main export terminal and refineries which supports competitiveness across price cycles.

Improving Capital Structure: A.I. Candelaria improved its capital structure through a 2025 tender offer and a USD65 million prepayment in the third quarter of 2025. The transaction reduced the outstanding 2028 bonds to USD179 million and lowered the 2H28 balloon payment to USD83 million from USD113 million, decreasing refinancing risk. Fitch expects gross leverage (total debt/dividends received) to be around 4.0x in 2025, with interest coverage exceeding 3.5x, reflecting stronger cash flow coverage versus prior expectations.

Structural Subordination: A.I. Candelaria's notes remain structurally subordinated to OCENSA's outstanding USD400 million senior notes. As the holding company, A.I. Candelaria relies on dividends upstreamed from subsidiaries to meet its obligations. A material increase in leverage at OCENSA could heighten subordination risk. This is mitigated by OCENSA's record of stable dividend distributions and A.I. Candelaria's protective minority rights, including vetoes over dividend policy changes and capex above USD100 million. Fitch expects projected dividend stream to comfortably cover interest expense and scheduled principal payments on A.I. Candelaria's notes.

Strong Minority Rights: A shareholder agreement affords A.I. Candelaria significant influence on OCENSA's dividend policy and other key decisions. The company can appoint two of five board members and holds veto rights over significant matters. A 90.1% shareholder vote is required to change OCENSA'S dividend policy and other major business decisions, supporting cash flow visibility and liquidity at A.I. Candelaria.

Peer Analysis

A.I. Candelaria's ratings rely on the dividend stream from OCENSA, an entity with non-investment-grade credit quality supported by predictable operating performance typical of contracted midstream assets. OCENSA's credit profile remains linked to that of Ecopetrol through ownership, strategic alignment and operational integration which Fitch views as providing incentives for support if needed.

Regional tolling-based natural gas peers in the region, such as Transportadora de Gas Internacional S.A. E.S.P. (TGI; BBB/Negative) and Transportadora de Gas del Peru, S.A. (TGP; BBB+/Stable), benefit from more purely take-or-pay models that support higher leverage. OCENSA's stronger financial profile, with average leverage below 0.3x over the rating horizon, is balanced by a greater exposure to volumetric risk given its higher reliance on ship-and-pay contracts than peers.

Key Assumptions

--OCENSA's transported volumes for ship-and-pay contracts grow by 1% over the rating horizon;

--OCENSA's transported volumes for ship-or-pay contracts are conducted per negotiated terms with off-takers;

--Current tariffs remain valid through 2024 and then increase by 1% annually thereafter;

--Dividend payout of 100% of net income;

--Exchange rate as forecast by Fitch's Sovereign Group;

--Debt service reserve account covers 1.25x of the next debt service payment, covering interest and principal;

--Dividends distribution contingent on meeting the debt service reserve account requirement.

RATING SENSITIVITIES

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

--A downgrade of OCENSA's credit ratings;

--EBITDA interest coverage, measured as dividends received to gross interest expense, sustained below 2.5x;

--Significant additional debt at the OCENSA level, which increases the structural subordination of A.I. Candelaria;

--Failure to deleverage below 4.5x over the rating horizon, which could widen the rating differential with OCENSA.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

--An upgrade of OCENSA's credit ratings.

Liquidity and Debt Structure

Fitch expects liquidity to remain strong, supported by A.I. Candelaria's readily available cash and consistently positive FCF. The 2028 notes amortize in 12 consecutive semiannual installments beginning in 2022, totaling 70% of the issuance, with the remaining balance due at maturity. The 2033 notes amortize in 10 consecutive semiannual installments beginning in 2028, totaling 75% of the issuance, with the balance due at maturity. A debt service reserve account, pledged as part of the collateral package, provides an additional liquidity buffer and is required to cover no less than 1.25x of the next debt service payment, including both interest and principal.

Issuer Profile

A.I. Candelaria is a holding company whose main source of cash is the dividends received from its 27.4% ownership interest in OCENSA, the largest crude oil transportation company in Colombia.

data.title: Walmart Picks Insider to Take Over as Next CEO — WSJ
data.description:
By Sarah Nassauer and Gareth Vipers

Walmart said Friday that Chief Executive Doug McMillon would step down after over a decade in the role, a change at the top of the country's largest retailer and private employer as it navigates a complex consumer and government landscape.

John Furner, current chief executive of Walmart U.S., will succeed McMillon as CEO after the end of the company's fiscal year Feb. 1, the company said. He will be the company's fifth CEO since founder Sam Walton.

The 51-year-old Furner has served as president and CEO of Walmart U.S. since 2019, overseeing the company's largest operating segment and more than 4,600 stores. He began his Walmart career as an hourly associate in 1993 and has held leadership roles across merchandising, operations and sourcing. His father was also an executive at the company.

Furner's experience gives him a unique combination; both an affection for workers and a nose for innovation, wrote McMillon in a note to staff Friday. That balance and belief in the company's values will help him lead "with the vision and experience to guide our people through a new retail era fueled by innovation and AI," he wrote.

Furner thanked McMillon and praised his tenure, in a letter to Walmart employees, but said he was also focused on his current role. "As we head into the holiday season, my priority is leading the U.S. segment to a strong finish," he said.

McMillon, 59 years old, has led Walmart through a period of fast growth as the retail behemoth remade its operations to focus more on e-commerce sales in the face of competition from Amazon. McMillon let the company's board know he planned to retire Tuesday and they voted to appoint Furner as CEO Thursday, the company said in a financial filing. McMillon will remain on Walmart's board through next spring and act as a company adviser through January 2027.

Walmart shares were down 2% in morning trade Friday.

As a longtime CEO with an obvious cast of potential internal successors, McMillon's exit has been rumored for years and in 2023 he planned to stay on for three more years, according to a Wall Street Journal article at the time. But the company hadn't indicated recently that a CEO transition was imminent.

Under McMillon's tenure, Walmart shares have risen more than 400% on a total return basis and the company has gained $576 billion in market capitalization, according to a Wall Street Journal analysis. It has also grown to over $680 billion in annual revenue.

When McMillon took the CEO job in 2014 Walmart was struggling. Its sales were stagnant and it had grown so big that its own executives doubted it could keep growing. Its workers were unhappy and its stores messy.

McMillon crafted a plan to raise hourly worker wages and invest more in stores and a digital transformation to fend off Amazon. In 2016, McMillon led Walmart's purchase of e-commerce startup Jet.com for $3.3 billion and placed its founder Marc Lore in charge of U.S. e-commerce operations. E-commerce sales took off, but many of the e-commerce projects of that era were eventually shuttered, including Jet.com.

In recent years, McMillon has worked to diversify Walmart's sources of revenue by pushing into advertising and memberships. He has also waded more than previous CEOs into the American political landscape and focused on improving Walmart's reputation. Walmart now pays workers above average compared with retail competitors, not near the bottom, and offers more benefits, McMillon said in a recent interview. Walmart's profits dipped as the strategy unfolded, but its revenue grew.

"There have been times when some people doubted us," McMillon said in his letter to staff. "Those people underestimated how much you care, and they underestimated your ability to change."

McMillon is well liked inside Walmart's Bentonville, Ark., headquarters and beyond. He is known for his gentle touch as a manager who has an ability to connect with everyone from store workers in Oklahoma to Silicon Valley startup founders. He has also visited President Trump at the White House several times this year to work to smooth over issues such as tariff policy.

That makes McMillon a hard act to follow.

Over a long career with Walmart, Furner has held top executive roles at Walmart China and the company's warehouse chain Sam's Club. Like McMillon, he attended the University of Arkansas and has deeply immersed himself in the company's culture and is close with the family of Walmart founder Sam Walton who hold two board seats and significant stockholdings. But, unlike McMillon, Furner is known to have a rougher touch as a leader. Furner embraces new technology and ideas and sometimes ruffles feathers, say people familiar with the situation.

Walmart faces a challenging landscape as Furner takes over. Its sales grew rapidly throughout the pandemic as shoppers scrambled for food and toilet paper, and then as inflation made its low-priced groceries and homegoods appealing to many shoppers. Walmart and Amazon have become behemoths vacuuming up market share and challenging competitors.

Now Walmart faces an evolving landscape due to artificial intelligence and uncertain consumer demand. In recent months Walmart has hired a head of AI acceleration and McMillon has said the company is bracing for AI to "to change literally every job ."

A sign of how significant Walmart executives believe AI will be, the company recently announced a shopping partnership with OpenAI to let shoppers buy its products directly within ChatGPT.

A search bar has long been the way shoppers browse and buy online, said McMillon last month. "That is about to change," he said.

data.title: September Jobs Report Coming Thursday After Government Reopens — WSJ
data.description:
By Harriet Torry and Matt Grossman

The reopened federal government will on Thursday release the first backlogged monthly jobs report, for the month of September. And a flood of other data is due to start flowing soon.

There is pent-up demand for new numbers after the longest shutdown on record — topping six weeks — delayed a critical batch of indicators that economists, analysts and investors track closely to judge the health of the economy. These include federal reports on the job market, inflation, economic growth and spending.

The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics announced the revised schedule Friday afternoon.

The jobs data are especially important because the health of a shaky U.S. labor market has become a growing concern. Federal Reserve officials have been divided during the data blackout over whether persistent inflation or the sluggish labor market is the greater economic threat, clouding the path for interest rate cuts.

The latest monthly jobs report, published Sept. 5, showed that employers created 22,000 new jobs in August and that the unemployment rate ticked up to 4.3%.

Write to Harriet Torry at harriet.torry@wsj.com and Matt Grossman at matt.grossman@wsj.com

data.title: ICE Review: Canola Weakens Near End of Session
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures erased earlier gains to end Friday in the red after declines in United States grains and oilseeds carried over into canola.

An analyst said that if January canola stayed above C$650 per tonne for the entire session, it would build up a "base of resistance". However, he also warned the contract was slightly overbought.

The Canadian Grain Commission reported canola exports for the week ended Nov. 9 were 121,200 tonnes, down from the previous week's 188,400. The year-to-date total reached 1.54 million tonnes, compared to 3.36 million the same time last year.

Chicago soyoil and European rapeseed were down and Malaysian palm oil was mixed. Crude oil gained more than US$1 per barrel after Ukrainian drones attacked a major Russian oil depot last night.

At mid-afternoon, the Canadian dollar was up less than one-tenth of a U.S. cent compared to Thursday's close.

There were 43,882 canola contracts traded on Friday, compared to Thursday when 57,401 contracts changed hands. Spreading accounted for 22,480 of the contracts traded.

data.title: Oil Futures Rise After Ukraine Strikes Russian Port — Market Talk
data.description: Oil futures rise with WTI back at $60 after a Ukrainian drone strike on a Russian Black Sea port revives concerns about supply disruptions. "Geopolitical tensions remain elevated while oil flows continue largely unabated," Amarpreet Singh of Barclays says in a note. While noting expectations of a large oil surplus next year, Barclays sees demand growing largely in line with the long-term prepandemic trend, a view it says is bolstered by continued growth in Chinese demand. "Russian exports have remained resilient, largely in line with our view, but we sense a wide range of opinions on the potential effect of sanctions," Singh adds. WTI settles up 2.4% at $60.09 a barrel. Brent rises 2.2% to $64.39 a barrel.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: US - Flood diversion P3 bonds need amendment
data.description:
By Alison Healey

The Fargo-Moorhead Metropolitan Area flood risk management has run into construction disputes that could be settled if bondholders agree to an amendment that would resolve construction disputes and restore the project's original timeline, The Bond Buyer reported.

The project sponsor is Red River Valley Alliance, a consortium of Acciona, Shikun & Binui, and North American Construction Group. The consortium reached financial close on the project in 2021 and entered an agreement to design, build, finance, operate and maintain the flood diversion channel for 29 years following construction. RRVA requested that senior creditors consent to an amended project agreement and amended design and construction contract by end of business day on Friday. Senior noteholders, bondholders and senior lenders are entitled to cast votes, and two-thirds of them are required to consent in order for the amendment to be put in place.

The project was placed in the Municipal Market Analytics' impairment database due to an event notice posted on the Municipal Securities Rulemaking Board's Web site last month, according to the report.

A US$273.3m senior revenue green bond backed the US$2.75bn project. A US$125m tranche maturing in 2051 priced to yield 2.98% and a US$148.3m tranche maturing in 2056 priced at 3.08%. The tax-exempt bonds were issued by the Public Finance Authority, which will lend the proceeds to RRVA. The team will construct a 30-mile river diversion channel to protect the area from flooding along with other associated infrastructure including bridges, rail crossings and aqueducts. The project is led by the Army Corp of Engineers and the Metro Flood Diversion Authority in Fargo, North Dakota.

The private partner is to be compensated with construction milestone payments and availability payments. A P3 is being used to construct the diversion channel portion of the project, while the Army Corps of Engineers is using design-bid-build methods to complete the southern embankment. An additional US$200m bond, a US$586m revolving credit, and US$51m equity rounded out the private finance contribution. Moody’s assigned a Baa3 rating to the bonds.

Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - November 14, 2025) - Avondale is excited to announce its participation in the inaugural Canada Climate Week Xchange (CCWX). As part of this national initiative, Avondale will host CCWX X Avondale which aims to bring together leaders from finance, policy, and industry to discuss key themes shaping Canada’s climate and sustainable finance landscape. Avondale is proud to host five in-person panel discussions as part of the inaugural Canada Climate Week Exchange (November 24–28, 2025). The in-person sessions, being held on Wednesday, November 26, in Toronto, will cover topics including Unlocking Capital in Canada’s Building Transition, Cultivating Resilience: The Future of Agri-Food Finance, The Future of Sustainable Investing, Empowering SMEs in the Climate Action Landscape, and Accelerating EV Infrastructure Investment. Two virtual sessions will also be held: Bridging the Gap: Mobilising Institutional Capital with Green Guarantees (Nov 25) and Building Canada’s Carbon Capture Value Chain (Nov 28).

CCWX X Avondale is taking place during CCWX 2025 which runs from November 24 to November 30th.

data.title: Premier Air Charter Holdings Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Premier Air Charter Holdings Inc., a leading provider of private air charter services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational developments, reflecting the company's ongoing efforts to expand its fleet and enhance its service offerings.

Financial Highlights

Revenue: Premier Air Charter Holdings Inc. reported revenue of $7.07 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, a substantial increase from $4.02 million for the same period in 2024. This growth was primarily driven by increased charter revenue from the addition of new aircraft.

Gross Profit (Loss): The company recorded a gross loss of $0.45 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to a gross loss of $0.15 million for the same period in 2024. The higher loss reflects increased pre-charter operating costs.

Income (Loss) from Operations: The loss from operations was $1.49 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to a loss of $0.92 million for the same period in 2024. This increase was due to higher operating expenses and pre-charter costs.

Net Income (Loss): The net loss for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was $1.80 million, compared to a net loss of $1.18 million for the same period in 2024. The increased loss was impacted by higher interest expenses and operating costs.

Net Loss Per Basic and Diluted Share: The net loss per basic and diluted share remained consistent at $0.01 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, reflecting the impact of increased costs on earnings per share.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, the company reported disaggregated revenues with charter sales contributing $20,072,063, management fees $38,250, maintenance revenues $239,582, and other revenues $36,290.

Operational Performance: The company experienced a significant increase in charter revenue due to the addition of new aircraft to the fleet in 2025, contributing to a $5,080,126 increase in charter revenue.

Cost of Sales: The cost of sales increased significantly due to pre-charter revenue operating costs of acquired aircraft and increased flight expenses, reflecting the operational expansion.

Operating Expenses: There was an increase in operating expenses primarily due to higher consulting, professional, and marketing fees, as well as increased salaries to support expanded operations.

Future Outlook: Premier Air Charter Holdings Inc. plans to continue relying on related parties and seek additional financing to support business operations and future growth. The company aims to improve charter revenue and obtain cost-effective financing for additional aircraft.

data.title: Hestia Insight Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Hestia Insight Inc., a company transitioning from its legacy operations in vending services to high-growth areas in AI product development and IP-based consulting, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ended August 31, 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and strategic business shifts aimed at positioning Hestia Insight as a leader in AI-driven advisory services.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $8,000. The limited amount of revenue was due to the company's current emphasis on planning and preparation for future revenue.
  • Operating Income/(Loss): $(281,317). The increase in operating expenses was mainly driven by an increase in professional fees related to becoming and being a public company.
  • Net Income/(Loss): $(934,426). The net loss for the nine months ended August 31, 2025, was primarily due to the limited revenue and increased operating expenses.
  • Basic net income/(loss) per common share: $(0.03). Reflects the net loss per share for the period.
  • Diluted net income/(loss) per common share: $(0.03). Consistent with the basic net income/(loss) per common share.

Business Highlights

  • Vending Machine Operations Discontinued: During the quarter ended August 31, 2025, Hestia Vending, a wholly owned subsidiary, completed the sale and termination of its vending machine operations. The company sold its remaining vending machines and related equipment, fully discontinuing its vending and charging-station operations.
  • Strategic Shift to AI and IP-Based Consulting: Hestia Insight Inc. has shifted its focus from legacy operations in Healthy Vending Services and Portable Charging Network Business to high-growth areas in AI product development and IP-based consulting. This strategic move is aimed at becoming a leader in AI-driven advisory services.
  • Capital Raise for AI Expansion: The Board has approved a capital raise of up to $5 million through equity and/or convertible debt. The proceeds will be used to expand the AI development team, accelerate R&D, market AI consulting tools, and for general corporate purposes.
  • Focus on Healthcare and Biotech Sectors: The company continues to focus on the healthcare and biotech sectors through its subsidiaries, providing strategic consulting, medical supply sales support, management, and capital market advisory services.
  • Future Outlook: The company is positioned to make strategic acquisitions and enter joint ventures with emerging growth companies with unique sciences and technologies. It aims to provide sales and marketing guidance and capital markets advisory services to its clients.

SEC Filing: Hestia Insight Inc. [ HSTA ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: ATLANTIC AMERICAN CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Atlantic American Corp, a diversified holding company engaged in specialty insurance markets, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial and operational performance, driven by increased premium revenue and improved underwriting results across its business segments.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $53.8 million, reflecting an increase from $44.5 million in the prior year period, driven by higher premium revenue in both property and casualty operations and life and health operations.
  • Net Income: $0.6 million, compared to a net loss of $2.0 million in the prior year period, primarily due to increased premium revenue and unrealized gains on equity securities.
  • Net Income Per Share (basic and diluted): $0.02, compared to a loss of $0.10 per share in the prior year period, reflecting improved profitability.
  • Operating Income: $1.6 million, compared to an operating loss of $0.7 million in the prior year period, driven by increased premium revenue and improved underwriting results.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: American Southern's net earned premiums increased by 38.8% during the three months ended September 30, 2025, primarily due to a rate increase in the automobile liability line and increased premiums in the inland marine and automobile physical damage lines.
  • Revenue Segments: Bankers Fidelity's net earned premiums rose by 6.9% in the three months ended September 30, 2025, driven by new sales in the Medicare supplement and group accident and health lines.
  • Operational Performance: American Southern's combined ratio improved to 97.9% for the three months ended September 30, 2025, indicating an underwriting profit, compared to 109.8% in the same period in 2024.
  • Operational Performance: Bankers Fidelity achieved a combined ratio of 96.1% for the nine months ended September 30, 2025, reflecting improved underwriting results compared to 103.1% in the same period in 2024.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued growth in premium revenue, particularly in the automobile liability and Medicare supplement lines, supported by new sales and rate increases.
  • Future Outlook: Management expects the operational improvements and premium growth trends to positively impact the company's financial performance in the upcoming quarters.

SEC Filing: ATLANTIC AMERICAN CORP [ AAME ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Fed's Logan: Would be hard to support December rate cut
data.description:
Key points:
  • Logan did not support a rate cut in October
  • Labor market not now in need of further preemptive insurance, Logan says
  • Logan next votes on rate-setting next year

By Ann Saphir

Dallas Federal Reserve President Lorie Logan on Friday again signaled she would oppose an interest-rate cut in December, after also opposing the Fed's rate cut in October, because of her concern that inflation is too high, trending upward, and taking too long to get to the Fed's 2% target.

"As I look to the December meeting, I think it would be hard to support another rate cut unless we were to get convincing evidence that inflation is really coming down faster than my expectations or that we were seeing more than the gradual cooling that we've been seeing in the labor market," she said at an energy conference put on by the Dallas and Kansas City Fed banks.

Logan will not be a voter on rate-setting at the Fed until next year, though non-voters do participate in the Fed's monetary policy debate and can help shape the thinking at the table.

"Until I see convincing evidence that we are headed all the way back to our 2% target, I really do think modestly restrictive policy is appropriate," Logan said, in remarks that largely repeated comments she made days after the Fed's late-October decision to cut the policy rate to the 3.75%-4.00% range to support the labor market.

Logan said the job market is cooling, but gradually and appropriately so because the Fed is also trying to get inflation down and needs to keep short-term borrowing costs high enough to keep some braking power on the economy.

She said she supported the Fed's September rate cut as insurance against further labor-market weakening, but "it does not seem like a labor market to me that it would be appropriate for further preemptive insurance."

Instead she said the Fed can monitor the job market risks, "and if we saw more than gradual cooling then I would think it would be you know appropriate to consider another rate cut," she said.

data.title: Fitch Takes Various Rating Actions on Affirm 2024-X2; Assigns Positive Outlooks on Two Classes
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-New York-14 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has upgraded the class B and class C notes of Affirm Asset Securitization Trust 2024-X2 (AFFRM 2024-X2) to 'AA+sf' and 'A+sf' from 'AAsf' and 'Asf', respectively. In addition, Fitch has assigned Positive Rating Outlooks to the class B and class C notes, reflecting increased credit enhancement (CE) since closing and the expectation for CE to continue building. The class A ratings are affirmed with Rating Outlook Stable.

Affirm Asset Securitization Trust 2024-X2

----A 00833QAA3; Long Term Rating; Affirmed; AAAsf; Rating Outlook Stable

----B 00833QAB1; Long Term Rating; Upgrade; AA+sf; Rating Outlook Positive

----C 00833QAC9; Long Term Rating; Upgrade; A+sf; Rating Outlook Positive

Transaction Summary

AFFRM 2024-X2 is a static pool and has built CE since closing. Collateral characteristics for this review remain similar to closing and performance has been in line with our initial expectations. The class A note has paid down to a 17.5% note factor and the class B notes will start receiving principal payments once the class A notes are paid in full.

KEY RATING DRIVERS

Consistent Collateral Quality: The WA FICO for AFFRM 2024-X2 is 684, higher than 680 at closing. The pool consists entirely of Affirm's Core product, of which 48.3% are Affirm Loan Grade A loans, which have produced the lowest defaults historically, up from 42.8% at closing. Affirm Grade E loans, which have historically produced the highest defaults, continue to make up the smallest proportion of the pool, at only 1.03% of 2024-X2 marginally lower than 1.1% at the time of closing. The WA contract rate for the pool is 26.76% consistent with 26.94% at closing, the highest to date for the platform.

Stable Performance Trends: After analyzing performance trends since closing, Fitch assigned a base case default assumption of 9.00% for the remaining pool. After incorporating the defaults recognized since closing, this equated to a lifetime cumulative base case default assumption of 3.79%, down from 6.50% at closing. The WA base case at closing was established using Affirm's proprietary risk grade and loan term. Performance has been in line with expectations, with overall pool characteristics consistent from the time of closing.

Credit Enhancement Mitigates Stressed Losses: The current hard CE totals 66.62%, 47.26%, 30.48%, and 6.93%, respectively, compared to 28.58%, 21.43%, 15.23%, and 6.53%, respectively, for class A, B, C, and D notes at closing. The CE is sufficient to cover Fitch's stressed cash flow assumptions for all classes. Fitch applied a 'AAAsf' rating stress of 4.75x as base-case default rate for consumer loans. This has been revised downward from 5.0x applied at closing, reflecting familiarity with the company, stable performance on Affirm's X platform, and more data history.

The stress multiples decrease for lower rating levels according to Fitch's Consumer ABS Rating Criteria. The default multiple reflects the absolute value of the default assumption, the length of default performance history, the WA FICO score of the borrowers, and the length of the revolving period, which exposes the trust to the potential for performance degradation.

Assurance for True Lender Status for Partner Bank-Loan Origination: Affirm's securitization transactions involve consumer loans originated by three key partner banks, Cross River Bank (CRB), a New Jersey state-chartered commercial bank, Celtic Bank, a Utah state-chartered industrial bank, and Lead Bank, a Missouri state-chartered bank. The banks' true lender status in the context of Affirm's loan acquisition is subject to legal and regulatory uncertainty, especially if the loans' interest rates exceeded those allowed by the borrowers' state usury laws.

If a court ruling or regulatory action deems that Affirm, rather than CRB, Celtic Bank, Lead Bank, or another originating bank partner, is the true lender, loans might be declared unenforceable, void or be subject to interest rate reductions and other penalties. This would increase negative rating pressure. For a receivable to be considered eligible for the pool, it may not exceed the civil usury limit of its applicable state. Fitch's analysis and expected ratings reflect the view that Affirm's criteria for selecting the receivables for AFFRM 2024-X2 along with their compliance, legal, and operational capabilities mitigate the risk of Affirm being deemed the true lender.

Adequate Servicing Capabilities: Affirm has an acceptable record of servicing consumer loans. Affirm partners with several sub-servicers to manage customer care, first party collections, and third-party collections. All sub-servicers are trained with Affirm materials and on Affirm systems, and are subject to Affirm policies and procedures. In addition, the entity's credit-risk profile is mitigated by the inclusion of Nelnet Servicing, LLC as a backup servicer to ensure continuity in the servicing of loans. Fitch considers all parties to be adequate servicers for this pool of consumer loans.

RATING SENSITIVITIES Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

Unanticipated increases in the frequency of defaults or charge-offs could produce loss levels higher than the base case and would likely result in declines of CE and remaining net loss coverage levels available to the notes. Decreased CE may make certain ratings on the notes susceptible to potential negative rating actions, depending on the extent of the decline in coverage.

Fitch conducts sensitivity analysis by stressing a transaction's initial base case default assumption an additional 10%, 25%, and 50% and examining rating implications. These increases of the base-case default rate are intended to provide an indication of the rating sensitivity of the notes to unexpected deterioration of a trust's performance.

Rating sensitivity from increased defaults (class A/class B/class C):

Current Ratings: 'AAAsf'/'AAsf'/'Asf'.

Defaults increase 10%: 'AAAsf'/'AAAsf'/'AAsf';

Defaults increase 25%: 'AAAsf'/'AAAsf'/'AA-sf';

Defaults increase 50%: 'AAAsf'/'AAsf'/'Asf'.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

Stable to improved asset performance driven by stable delinquencies would lead to increasing CE levels and consideration for potential upgrades. If defaults are 25% less than the projected base case default rate, the expected ratings for the class B and C notes could be upgraded by up to three notches.

Rating sensitivity from decreased defaults (class A/class B/class C):

Current Ratings: 'AAAsf'/'AAsf'/'Asf'.

Defaults decrease 25%: 'AAAsf'/'AAAsf'/'AAAsf'.

USE OF THIRD PARTY DUE DILIGENCE PURSUANT TO SEC RULE 17G -10

Form ABS Due Diligence-15E was not provided to, or reviewed by, Fitch in relation to this rating action.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision.
